requested_file_note: Directory contains exactly one file, with a different date and topic. Analyzed that file instead — almost certainly the intended target.

evidence.actual_pattern: user → user → user → user → user (5 in a row, separated by '---')
evidence.every_visible_block_is_quoted_with_leadi: Yes — every block has '> ' prefix, which buildMarkdown only applies to messages whose role is 'human' (popup.js:543). Zero plain-text blocks → zero AI responses survived extraction.

root_cause.diagnosis: All four AI-side extraction strategies for Claude.ai fail against the current DOM, while the user-side strategy succeeds because [data-testid='user-message'] is stable. Result: every captured payload has correct user turns and zero assistant turns.
failing_selectors: sibling-walk fallback (extractClaude Strategy 1 else branch, content.js:66-85)
root_cause.why_user_messages_survive: content.js:29 uses '[data-testid="user-message"]' which Claude.ai still emits. User-turn extraction is fine.
root_cause.why_assistant_messages_are_missed: Claude.ai's current DOM either (a) does not emit data-testid on assistant turns, OR (b) places assistant turns in a parent/ancestor container rather than a direct sibling of the user turn. Either way, all four fallback strategies whiff. The 5+ assistant turns that visually existed on the page were never extracted.

ruled_out.cortex_backend_bug: Ruled out. http.ts:11 BODY_LIMIT=10MB (orders of magnitude above any conversation). Save path (main/index.ts:27-61) writes memory.content as-is — no parsing, no slicing.
ruled_out.domToMarkdown_truncation: Ruled out. content.js:281-369 walks the full DOM tree recursively; no length cap.
ruled_out.substring_in_extension: Ruled out. Only .slice() calls on text are: title truncation to 57 chars (background.js:130, TITLE only), filename slug to 50 chars (index.ts:43, FILENAME only), debug-log previews (content.js:166,215,263, console only).
ruled_out.dom_virtualization: Ruled out as primary cause. Virtualization would lose OLD off-screen turns; this loses ALL assistant turns regardless of position. Virtualization could be a secondary factor in very long chats but is not the explanation here.

confidence_rationale: The file's structural pattern (every block quoted with '> ', no plain-text blocks at all) is only producible by buildMarkdown if the messages array contains exclusively role='human'. Cross-referenced against content.js extractClaude — the user-side selector ('[data-testid="user-message"]') matches what we observed, and the failure of the four AI-side strategies cleanly explains the missing assistant turns.

immediate_user_action: Open a real Claude.ai conversation in Chrome.
Paste the output back to me. That gives the current AI-message data-testid (or proves there isn't one and we need a CSS class / aria-label / element-type fallback).

fix_scope_if_dom_sample_provided.test_strategy: Add a fixtures/claude-real-dom.html sample to tests/, write extractClaude unit tests that load the fixture and assert role + content for each turn.
fix_scope_if_dom_sample_provided.release_classification: v0.1.0-beta hotfix (v0.1.0.1) — data-loss-grade regression, ships outside the v0.2 schedule.

fix_scope_if_no_dom_sample.status: Blocked. The fix requires seeing what Claude.ai actually emits today; we can't guess selectors blind.

broader_v0.2_implications: ChatGPT (extractChatGPT) and Gemini (extractGemini) likely have the same class of fragility — DOM-scraping multi-provider AI chats is a moving target by design.

Recommend P0 #2 (smart capture filtering) absorbs a small 'extraction-fidelity test harness' subtask: maintain provider-specific DOM fixtures, add a CI test that fails when extraction returns zero turns of either role.
v0.2 effort estimate for P0 #2 should rise from 6-9 hrs to 8-11 hrs to cover this.

v0_2_roadmap_impact.is_blocker_rationale: v0.2 work can proceed in parallel — this is an extension-only fix and v0.2 P0 #1 (dedup schema) is backend-only. They don't touch the same files.
v0_2_roadmap_impact.recommended_handling: Treat as a hotfix track running alongside v0.2: extract this fix into a small v0.1.0.1 release once the DOM sample arrives, OR fold into P0 #2 if you'd rather batch.
